name: Natural Park of Sierra and Cañones de Guara – Pools of Belsué and Steps of Flumen
name_original: Parque Natural de la Sierra y los Cañones de Guara – Pozas de Belsué y Gradas del Flumen
region_intro: Hiking around Nueno, located in the Hoya de Huesca region of Aragon, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The area is characterized by the dramatic mountainous backdrop of the Sierra de Gratal and includes a significant portion of the Sierra y los Cañones de Guara Natural Park, known for its pre-Pyrenean relief and extensive canyon systems. The Isuela River basin, along with features like the distinctive rocky formation of Salto de Roldán and the serene Gorgas de San Julián, contribute to the varied terrain. Hikers can explore paths ranging from gentle riverbanks to challenging mountain bluffs. Best hiking trails around Nueno The most popular hiking route is Gorges of San Julián Trail, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through the captivating Gorgas de San Julián. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tozal de Guara Loop from Nocito, a difficult 13.3 miles (21.4 km) path. This challenging loop offers extensive views of the mountainous terrain around Tozal de Guara. Local hikers also love the Salto de Roldán - Peña San Miguel Trail, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail leading through the iconic Salto de Roldán formation, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Hiking around Nueno is defined by rugged mountains, deep canyons, and serene river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Nueno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Nueno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Nueno?
A: Nueno offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 45 routes documented on komoot. These range from gentle walks along riverbanks to challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra y los Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Nueno?
A: The hiking trails around Nueno are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to serene river valleys, and the opportunity to explore iconic natural formations like the Salto de Roldán.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Nueno?
A: Yes, Nueno has several easy trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. There are 6 easy routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937775690/salto-de-roldan-pena-san-miguel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Roldán - Peña San Miguel Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.6 miles (4.2 km) long and takes about 1 hour 24 minutes, offering stunning views of the iconic rock formation.

Q: Can families with children find suitable hikes around Nueno?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Nueno are suitable for families, especially those looking for moderate distances and manageable elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933082177/gorges-of-san-julian-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of San Julián Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.8-mile (9.4 km) moderate route, is a popular choice, leading through captivating natural gorges that can be an exciting discovery for children.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Nueno area?
A: Yes, circular routes are a great way to experience the diverse scenery without retracing your steps. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14943002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nocito Village – Puente de Nocito loop from Nocito&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7-mile (11.3 km) trail that takes you through charming landscapes and offers a full loop experience.

Q: Where can I find trails with impressive natural landmarks or viewpoints?
A: Nueno is rich in natural landmarks. For dramatic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938296160/tozal-de-guara-loop-from-nocito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de Guara Loop from Nocito&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive vistas of the mountainous terrain. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgas de San Julián&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible via scenic circular routes, providing spaces for quiet contemplation amidst striking natural beauty.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water elements like gorges or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region around Nueno is characterized by its water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932406946/natural-park-of-sierra-and-canones-de-guara-pools-of-belsue-and-steps-of-flumen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Sierra and Cañones de Guara – Pools of Belsué and Steps of Flumen&lt;/a&gt; trail offers an easy walk along the Isuela River basin. Additionally, the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; are popular spots for nature lovers, often surrounded by hiking paths.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore while hiking near Nueno?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Nueno's surroundings offer historical insights. You can visit sites like the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Losa Mora&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful Abandoned Sanctuary of San Urbez&lt;/a&gt;, or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5003040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nocito Village&lt;/a&gt;, which is a starting point for several trails. These sites provide a cultural dimension to your outdoor explorations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Nueno?
A: The diverse landscape of Nueno makes it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery or rich fall colors. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal, especially for higher altitude trails. Winter hikes are also possible, particularly on lower elevation paths, but require appropriate gear for potentially colder conditions.

Q: Are there dog-friendly trails in Nueno?
A: Many trails in the Nueno area are suitable for hiking with dogs, allowing you to explore the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Sierra y los Cañones de Guara Natural Park, and to respect local wildlife and other hikers.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Nueno offers difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938296160/tozal-de-guara-loop-from-nocito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de Guara Loop from Nocito&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, a difficult 13.3-mile (21.4 km) path that involves over 1200 meters of ascent and descent, providing a strenuous but rewarding experience with extensive mountain views.
tips: The Flumen River generates a series of stepped terraces as it passes through Belsué and creates some pleasant pools below where it is possible to bathe.

Alberto Carneiro
"As flowering trees in Huesca", 2006
CDAN Art and Nature Collection-itinerary. Art and Nature Center. Beulas Foundation. Huesca

For more information: cdan.es/arte-y-naturaleza/alberto-carneiro-as-arvores-florescem-em-huesca

waypoints.name: The Trees Bloom in Huesca (Alberto Carneiro, 2006)
